entities.name: Reframing Rejection Pattern
entities.context: Voice primitive that reframes behavior as values-driven rather than deficient: 'not rejecting X, rejecting [adjective] Y'

entities.name: Values Alignment
entities.context: The idea that employee behavior interpreted as laziness is actually alignment with different values of success

entities.name: Outdated Definitions of Success
entities.context: Legacy success metrics that employees are pushing back against, per V's framing

entities.name: Growth
entities.context: Professional/organizational growth that V argues people are not actually rejecting

entities.name: Leadership
entities.context: Domain where the reframing primitive applies

entities.name: Culture
entities.context: Domain where the reframing primitive applies

entities.name: Management
entities.context: Domain where the reframing primitive applies

entities.name: LinkedIn
entities.context: Source platform where this voice primitive was published

entities.name: Vrijen Attawar
entities.context: Author of the voice primitive and reframing pattern

relationships.context: V uses this rhetorical pattern as a high-distinctiveness (0.90) voice primitive in external writing
The pattern reframes apparent rejection of growth as a statement of values alignment
The primitive positions outdated success definitions as the real target of rejection
Growth is the thing people are NOT rejecting in V's framing
Leadership is a primary domain for applying this reframing
Culture discourse is a domain for this primitive
Management contexts benefit from this reframing of employee behavior
LinkedIn is the source platform where this primitive was captured
Values alignment is the positive reframe; outdated success definitions are what's actually being rejected
